user_message: [AUTOMATION TRIGGER] Daily automation: Calendar event with non-Microsoft external attendee detected. Account: Pacific Northwest Outfitters.

Date: 2024-02-11 09:50 EST
Subject: URGENT: deployment blockers Issue at Pacific Northwest Outfitters

From: Andrew Taylor <andrew.taylor@pacificnorth.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-01 14:57 EST
Subject: URGENT: deployment blockers Issue at Pacific Northwest Outfitters

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about deployment blockers. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

Meeting: deployment blockers Discussion - Pacific Northwest Outfitters
Date: 2024-03-06
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Andrew Taylor (Pacific Northwest Outfitters), Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft)

[00:01:30] Andrew Taylor (Pacific Northwest Outfitters): Thanks everyone for joining. Let's dive into deployment blockers.

[00:08:00] Andrew Taylor (Pacific Northwest Outfitters): Let me add some context -- we've been working on budget approval and found that there's a dependency on the security team's review that could delay us.

[00:10:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): The timeline for vendor dependencies is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:14:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): On the deployment blockers front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around technical validation.

[00:19:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): On the deployment blockers front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around technical validation.

[00:22:00] Andrew Taylor (Pacific Northwest Outfitters): Let me add some context -- we've been working on resource allocation and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

[00:24:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): On the deployment blockers front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around data residency.

[00:27:00] Andrew Taylor (Pacific Northwest Outfitters): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about vendor dependencies. Can we address those?

[00:32:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): On the deployment blockers front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around security compliance.

[00:36:00] Andrew Taylor (Pacific Northwest Outfitters): The timeline for budget approval is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:40:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): The timeline for timeline constraints is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.
